Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of San Franciscos Petition To Compel Enforcement Of Subpoena Issued To Homeaway.Com Inc. By The San Francisco Tax Collector
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorney, James M. Emery of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-4628) appears as counsel for the petitioner

Attorney, James C. Grant of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (206-622-3150) appears telephonically as counsel for respondent/opposing party Homeaway.com

Attorney, Sanjay Nangia of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (415-276-6500) appears telephonically as counsel for respondent/opposing party Homeaway.com

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

PETITIONERS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO TREASURER JOSE CISNEROS AND SAN FRANCISCO TAX COLLECTOR DAVID AUGUSTINE'S Petition To Compel Enforcement Of Subpoena Issued To Homeaway.Com Inc. By The San Francisco Tax Collector is GRANTED. This action is stayed 60 days to allow respondent to file appellate brief. Court states findings in open forum as set forth inthe transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Court finds insufficient support in the record to establish that Homeaway is either an "ECS" or "RCS." There's also insufficient evidence to show that this matter was burdensome. Court does not believe the subpoena's to be overbroad. Court does not believe it was violative of the first
amendment rights nor that it needs to reach the issue of the City's authority under the hotel tax. Court believes the subpoena is sufficiently related to the tax ordinances to permit
the subpoena to be enforced. Counsel for the City, Mr. Emery shall submit a [proposed] written order tracking verbatim the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Off Calendar Aug-25-2016 Continued To Sep-20-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorney, Mark D. Lipton (415-554-4218) appears as counsel for petitioner

Attorneys Sanjay M. Nangia and James C. Grant of Davis Wright Tremain. LLP (415-276-6577) appear as counsels for respondent/opposing party

Court makes disclusore regarding Homeaway.com Inc. No objection; the hearing proceeds. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

PETITIONERS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO TREASURER JOSE CISNEROS AND SAN FRANCISCO TAX COLLECTOR DAVID AUGUSTINE'S Petition To Compel Enforcement Of Subpoena Issued To Homeaway.Com Inc. By The San Francisco Tax Collector is CONTINUED FOR RULING ONLY on September 20, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 302. Each side has leave to submit citations to authority on the issue of whether c(1)(e) allows for information sought by the the treasurer in subpoena. The parties may submit authorities via email at contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org. (D302)
